method: minimax MCP websearch + WebFetch of primary docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com, github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent)

mappings.hermes_equivalent: hermes chat -q "<prompt>"  (one-shot, non-interactive)  OR  hermes -z "<prompt>"  (scripted one-shot: strips ALL cosmetic output, final-text-only on stdout)
mappings.notes: hermes chat -q runs a single query non-interactively and includes tool output in transcript. hermes -z is the bare-bones variant: 'single prompt in, final response text out, nothing else on stdout or stderr'. If you need tool output in transcript, use hermes chat -q. Both documented as the canonical one-shot replacement for claude -p. Source: CLI guide (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/cli).

mappings.hermes_equivalent: hermes chat -q "<prompt>" --output-format json   (structured JSON: result text + session_id, model, provider, num_turns, total_cost_usd, usage). NOTE: a dedicated stream-json / --include-partial-messages flag is NOT documented for hermes chat; the closest equivalents are (a) --output-format json for structured one-shot output (proposed in issue #3326, partial support), and (b) hermes sessions export --session-id <id>  for POST-HOC full trajectory including tool_use messages.
mappings.notes: Hermes does not expose a real-time stream-json NDJSON pipe equivalent. The Claude-Code skill page (bundled in Hermes itself) documents claude's --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages, but those flags belong to the child claude process, not to hermes chat. For streaming Hermes's own output, the documented path is hermes chat -Q (--quiet) to suppress banner/spinner, plus --output-format json when available. For post-run tool-call history use hermes sessions export (see session_inspection_verdict.json).

mappings.hermes_equivalent: hermes chat --model "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"  OR  hermes chat -m "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"  (override the configured default model; --provider can also force a specific provider)
mappings.notes: Both --model and -m are documented as model override flags. The cli-config.yaml.example explicitly notes 'Default model to use (can be overridden with --model flag).' Provider forcing is via --provider (nous | openrouter | anthropic, etc.).

mappings.claude_pattern: os.environ.pop('CLAUDECODE', None)  (env-strip for nesting Claude inside Claude)
mappings.hermes_equivalent: NO documented equivalent. Hermes does NOT auto-strip CLAUDECODE because CLAUDECODE is an Anthropic-specific signal. The equivalent Hermes-aware env vars are: HERMES_SESSION_ID (set automatically; do NOT override), HERMES_HOME (override config dir, useful for isolated nested profile), HERMES_YOLO_MODE (bypass approval prompts). For isolation between parent and child Hermes sessions use the profile mechanism (hermes -p <name>) which gives each profile its own HERMES_HOME, sessions, and gateway PID.
mappings.notes: Env-vars reference explicitly says 'You should not set HERMES_SESSION_ID manually'. For nesting Hermes-in-Hermes, profiles (-p <name>) are the documented isolation boundary; they isolate ~/.hermes, sessions, gateway PID, and systemd service name. The CLAUDECODE strip pattern does not apply to Hermes.

Official docs live at hermes-agent.nousresearch.com (Mintlify). Source markdown in github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/tree/main/website/docs. The repo README explicitly states 'All documentation lives at hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs'.

mappings.claude_pattern: Anthropic NDJSON event shape  {type: ..., message: {content: [{type, text}]}}  (claude -p --output-format stream-json event stream)
mappings.hermes_equivalent: NO direct equivalent. Hermes does not emit the Anthropic-style NDJSON event stream. The closest Hermes-side event sources are: (a) hermes chat -q --output-format json — single final result object (not streaming, not NDJSON), (b) hermes sessions export --session-id <id> — full post-hoc trajectory in ShareGPT-compatible JSONL with tool_use turns inlined as  ```{'role': 'assistant', 'content': '... ```{'name': ..., 'arguments': {...}}``` ...'}``` inside the gpt turn (arguments are JSON objects, not strings).
mappings.notes: Confirmed by trajectory-format docs: 'Assistant turn embeds ```{'name': ..., 'arguments': {...}}``` — arguments are JSON objects, not strings (parsed back from the API's JSON-string form). Tool turn wraps each result as <tool_response>{...}</tool_response>.' So Hermes's JSONL shape is ShareGPT-flavored, not Anthropic NDJSON. Schema mismatch is real; do not assume consumers of claude NDJSON can read this.

mappings.hermes_equivalent: NO direct equivalent. Hermes slash commands are CODE-defined via CommandDef in hermes_cli/commands.py — there is no user-editable .hermes/commands/ directory. Closest analogues: (a) skills installed to ~/.hermes/skills/ (or $HERMES_HOME/skills/) — preloadable via hermes -s <skill> or /skill <name> mid-session; (b) project-context files (.hermes.md / HERMES.md / AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md) loaded with first-match-wins; (c) ~/.hermes/config.yaml for quick-command customization.
mappings.notes: Skills are loaded from ~/.hermes/skills/ (per-profile via HERMES_HOME) and may be installed from the hub via hermes skills install <id>. Project context is loaded from .hermes.md > HERMES.md > AGENTS.md > CLAUDE.md (capped at 20k chars). The registry of record for slash commands is hermes_cli/commands.py — adding one requires a code change, not a new file.

mappings.hermes_equivalent: NO direct equivalent. Hermes Cowork / Claude Cowork 1:1 product does not exist. Hermes provides subagent delegation via the delegate_task tool (signature: goal, context, toolsets, tasks, max_iterations, role) which spawns child AIAgent instances with isolated context, restricted toolsets, and own terminal sessions. Multi-session parallelism is also available via hermes --background (spawn isolated background sessions, no shared history) and parallel batch mode through delegate_task tasks=[...].
mappings.notes: Cowork is positioned as a managed autonomous desktop product; Hermes is CLI/gateway-first and open-source. Hermes covers the delegation use case via the tool-call layer (delegate_task) rather than a separate product surface. Per the Vellum and Flowtivity comparisons, Cowork and Hermes are explicitly different products with overlapping capability; no Hermes Cowork SKU exists.

mappings.claude_pattern: Anthropic subagent YAML format  {name, description, tools, prompt}  (Claude Code .claude/agents/*.md frontmatter)
mappings.hermes_equivalent: Hermes SKILL.md frontmatter is the analogue: each skill at ~/.hermes/skills/<name>/SKILL.md declares its name, description, and exposes its tools (each tool is a separate file under the skill directory). Multi-agent delegation itself is invoked from the agent loop via the delegate_task tool call: delegate_task(goal: str, context: str, toolsets: list[str], tasks: list, max_iterations: int, role: 'leaf' | 'orchestrator'). Subagents do NOT have a separate YAML manifest; they are spawned dynamically by delegate_task with a restricted toolset.
mappings.notes: Hermes skill frontmatter (name + description) is injected into the system prompt. Tool definitions live inside the skill directory. delegate_task is the runtime primitive that produces an isolated child AIAgent; the 'YAML manifest' analogue is the toolsets list passed at call time, not a static file.

mappings.hermes_equivalent: Python stdlib unchanged: import webbrowser; webbrowser.open(url). Hermes has its own browser-automation layers on top (Browser-Use skill, Pinchtab skill, Firecrawl, Camoufox, page-agent skill), but for the trivial 'open URL in default browser' use case Python stdlib webbrowser is still the canonical, simplest path — Hermes does not provide or require a special CLI for that.
mappings.notes: webbrowser is Python stdlib — no migration needed. Hermes-side, browser control lives in optional skills (page-agent, browser-use, Pinchtab issue #493). The point of the mapping is to flag that nothing needs to change for the trivial case; only when the skill needs an actual browser-driving agent does Hermes-specific machinery matter.

no_equivalent: claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages  (Hermes has --output-format json for one-shot, but no real-time stream-json / partial-messages pipe)
Anthropic NDJSON event shape  {type, message: {content: [{type, text}]}}  (Hermes emits ShareGPT-flavored JSONL via sessions export, not Anthropic NDJSON)
.claude/commands/<target>.md  (Hermes slash commands are code-defined in hermes_cli/commands.py, not user-editable files)
Anthropic Cowork  (no Hermes product called Cowork; subagent delegation via delegate_task is the closest analogue)
schema_mismatches_to_watch: Anthropic NDJSON vs Hermes ShareGPT JSONL: consumers of claude's stream-json CANNOT read hermes sessions export directly
delegate_task returns structured final summary, NOT intermediate tool traces (parent sees only child's final text)
HERMES_SESSION_ID is read-only (auto-set); do NOT pop/set it the way CLAUDECODE is stripped for claude nesting
